source_pr.title: *: ref all the jobs before sending to jobToWorkerCh
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #64666

Problem Summary:
In the generateAndSendJob function, we previously referenced one region job at a time and immediately sent it to jobToWorkerCh. If a job (especially an empty job) finished before the next job was referenced, the reference count could drop to 0, causing memoryIngestData to be cleaned unexpectedly. This prevented other jobs still being processed from accessing the data, leading to data inconsistency.
Only the partition table might meet this issue. Because we group keys by index id, not partition id, some ranges might cross many partitions, and the created region job might be empty and trigger this issue

### What changed and how does it work?
We modified the logic in generateAndSendJob to reference all jobs before sending them to jobToWorkerCh. Specifically, we first call job.ref(jobWg) for all generated jobs in a loop, and then send them to the channel one by one in a separate loop.

This ensures all jobs have their reference counts incremented before any of them are sent to the channel. Even if some jobs (like empty jobs) finish quickly and call done(), as long as other jobs still hold references, ingestData will not be cleaned prematurely. Only when all jobs complete and call done() will the reference count reach 0, at which point ingestData can be safely cleaned, preventing data inconsistency issues.

### Check List

- [x] Integration test
Pass the tidb-x HA add index test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Local backend Lightning tests were flaky because some region jobs could finish before all jobs were reference-counted, causing shared ingest data to be cleaned too early and leading to nondeterministic data inconsistency.
failure_signature: lightning local backend: intermittent failure when ingest data is cleaned early due to job ref-count race (empty region job finishes before other jobs ref it)
root_cause_explanation: The flaky behavior came from a logical race in Lightning local backend job scheduling: `generateAndSendJob` referenced one region job at a time and sent it immediately to `jobToWorkerCh`. If an (especially empty) job finished quickly, it could `done()` and drop the ref-count to 0, so the shared `memoryIngestData` got cleaned while other jobs were still being processed. That made later jobs observe missing data and produced nondeterministic failures. This PR fixes it by ref-counting *all* generated jobs first, and only then sending them to the worker channel, so ingest data is kept alive until all jobs complete.
fix_pattern: Prevent ingest-data premature cleanup by reference-counting all generated region jobs before sending them to the worker channel (`jobToWorkerCh`), so shared ingest data stays alive until all jobs complete.
analysis:
PR title: *: ref all the jobs before sending to jobToWorkerCh
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/64767
Triage sources: manual_triage, determinism_stabilization
Changed files (3): pkg/lightning/backend/external/engine.go, pkg/lightning/backend/local/local.go, pkg/lightning/backend/local/local_test.go
Manual review: Lightning local backend can clean shared ingest data too early if an empty region job finishes before all jobs are ref-counted. The fix ref-counts all jobs before publishing them to workers, avoiding this async race.
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
